title: circaea lutetiana subsp. quadrisulcata
intro: Circaea lutetiana subsp. quadrisulcata, also known as the marsh marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The marsh marigold is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, and it has been used to treat various ailments, including rheumatism and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Circaea lutetiana subsp. quadrisulcata include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other medicinal products.
